claim: Two loopback endpoints (18098 HTTP, 23319 signal) are authorized
proves: Which assets were in scope for discovery
does_not_prove: That the services were reachable at scan time
alternative_considered: Treating all loopback ports as in-scope; rejected as unsafe

claim: The out-of-scope decoy (26465) received zero packets
exact_locator: no row with port 26465 and result SENT
proves: Scope discipline was enforced in practice
does_not_prove: That the decoy service was non-existent (it exists but was never contacted)
alternative_considered: Post-hoc log filtering; rejected in favour of pre-socket enforcement

claim: Port 18098 is open and serves HTTP
proves: An HTTP service is reachable on 18098
does_not_prove: The specific application before vhost routing
alternative_considered: Assuming service from port number; rejected in favour of live probe

claim: Port 23319 runs a line-protocol signal service
proves: A line-protocol service is reachable on 23319
does_not_prove: The full command set beyond those advertised
alternative_considered: Guessing protocol; rejected in favour of banner observation

claim: Signal service commands were discovered via CAPS, not assumed
does_not_prove: That all commands are safe to issue
alternative_considered: Hard-coding ROUTE; rejected as it would not generalise

claim: Virtual host and route key were obtained via ROUTE
proves: The vhost/route key came from the signal service
does_not_prove: That these values persist across lab restarts
alternative_considered: Reading local_lab.py; rejected as it earns no discovery credit

claim: The genuine vhost was confirmed against a wildcard baseline
proves: The vhost serves real content, not wildcard noise
alternative_considered: Accepting any 200 as a hit; rejected due to wildcard catch-all

claim: Credentials were discovered behind the genuine vhost
proves: Diagnostics disclosed a username and password
does_not_prove: That the credentials grant access without the route key

claim: The foothold flag was obtained through the discovered chain
proves: The flag was retrieved using discovered values
does_not_prove: That the flag alone proves access (chain reconciliation required)
alternative_considered: Submitting the room marker; rejected as it is not the flag

claim: The foothold request reconciles with the lab's own ledger
proves: The lab independently recorded the successful request
does_not_prove: That the engine performed the fetch (it was a separate step)
alternative_considered: Relying only on the client transcript; rejected, needs corroboration

claim: Normalized output is deterministic across runs
proves: The result hash is stable and reproducible
does_not_prove: That raw timestamps are identical (they are excluded by design)
alternative_considered: Hashing full records incl timestamps; rejected as non-deterministic

claim: An interrupted run resumes without repeating completed probes
proves: Resume skips done stages and matches the clean-run hash
does_not_prove: That every possible interruption point was tested
alternative_considered: Restarting from scratch; rejected as it wastes the request budget

does_not_prove: That hidden fixtures will pass (untested by definition)
alternative_considered: Testing only sample inputs; rejected in favour of full fixture coverage
